title: hakea lorea subsp. borealis
intro: Hakea lorea subsp. borealis, a subspecies of the Hakea lorea plant, is a flowering shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and narrow, linear leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in Western Australia for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Hakea lorea subsp. borealis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in a topical cream or ointment to treat skin conditions.
